sections.content: The player moved DOWN x 4. Let me check the current state to see where the player is now and what the W bar position is.

sections.label: TOOL CALL: python
sections.content:
<tool_call>
<function=python>
<parameter=code>
# Check current state
seg = current_frame.segmentation
for n in seg['nodes']:
    if n['color'] in ['N', 'W', 'b', 'c', 'g', 'G']:
        rows = [p[0] for p in n['boundary']]
        cols = [p[1] for p in n['boundary']]
        print(f"{n['color']}: rows {min(rows)}-{max(rows)}, cols {min(cols)}-{max(cols)}, pixels={n['pixels']}")

# Check ascii around player and frames
ascii_lines = current_frame.ascii.split('\n')
print("\nRegion (rows 20-35, cols 22-42):")
for i in range(20, 35):
    print(f"{i:2d}: {ascii_lines[i][22:42]}")
</parameter>
</function>
</tool_call>

The player moved DOWN to rows 36-38, cols 24-27. The W bar is at row 39, cols 24-27 (BELOW the player). So the W bar is now on the BOTTOM side.
The dark gray frame at rows 20-23, cols 28-31 converted back to charcoal (c)! So the dark gray frame reverted to charcoal.
Also, the bottom bar at rows 60-63 changed. Previously it was at cols 5-63, now it's at cols 4-63. So it expanded 1 col to the LEFT.
Now the player is at rows 36-38, and the charcoal frames are at rows 20-23 and rows 24-27. The W bar is on the BOTTOM side.
I need to get the W bar to point RIGHT to shoot the charcoal frames. To do that, I need to move RIGHT and get blocked.
But the player is at rows 36-38, which is below the charcoal frames. If I move RIGHT, I won't hit the charcoal frames because they're above me.
Let me think about this differently. The W bar switches sides based on the player's movement direction when blocked. To get the W bar to point RIGHT, I need to move RIGHT and get blocked by something.
Looking at the ascii, the bottom frame is at rows 28-31, cols 28-39. The player is at rows 36-38, cols 24-27. So the bottom frame is above and RIGHT of the player. If I move RIGHT, I won't hit the bottom frame because it's above me.
Actually, there's no obstacle to the RIGHT of the player at rows 36-38. So I can move RIGHT freely.
Let me try moving RIGHT x 4 to get to cols 28-31, and then see what happens. The bottom frame is at cols 28-39, but it's at rows 28-31, which is above the player. So moving RIGHT won't hit the bottom frame.
Actually, wait. Let me reconsider the game mechanics. The W bar seems to switch sides based on the player's movement direction when blocked. But the player can also move freely in open space.
Looking at the ascii, the player is at rows 36-38, cols 24-27. The bottom frame is at rows 28-31, cols 28-39. So the bottom frame is above and RIGHT of the player. There's no obstacle to the RIGHT of the player at rows 36-38.
If I move RIGHT, the player will move to cols 28-31, and the W bar will stay on the BOTTOM side (or switch to RIGHT if blocked).
Actually, I think the W bar switches sides based on the player's last movement direction. When I moved DOWN, the W bar switched to the BOTTOM side. When I move RIGHT, the W bar might switch to the RIGHT side.
Let me try moving RIGHT x 4 and see what happens.
